skill.name: Azure Machine Learning SDK v2 for Python
skill.description: Azure Machine Learning SDK v2 for Python. Use for ML workspaces, jobs, models, datasets, compute, and pipelines.
skill.summary: Manage Azure ML resources including workspaces, training jobs, models, data assets, and compute clusters using the Azure ML SDK v2.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and code examples for Azure ML SDK v2. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines, finding no executable code or security patterns. The skill provides legitimate guidance for Azure ML resource management with no dangerous patterns detected.

content.user_title: Build and deploy ML solutions with Azure
content.value_statement: Azure ML projects require careful management of workspaces, compute, data, and models. This skill provides authoritative guidance for using Azure ML SDK v2 to streamline your machine learning workflow.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage Azure ML workspaces with proper configuration
Register and version data assets, models, and environments
Configure compute clusters with auto-scaling for training workloads
Submit and monitor command jobs and pipeline workflows
Organize resources with tags for cost tracking and governance
limitations: Requires valid Azure subscription and appropriate IAM permissions
Does not execute code - provides guidance for SDK usage only
Assumes Azure ML SDK v2 is installed in your environment
Examples use Python - other language SDKs not covered

use_cases.title: MLOps Engineer Setting Up Training Pipeline
use_cases.description: Configure compute clusters, register training data, submit pipeline jobs, and track model versions for production ML workflows.

use_cases.title: Data Scientist Running Experiments
use_cases.description: Create isolated workspaces, manage experiment data assets, submit training jobs, and compare model performance metrics.

use_cases.title: Cloud Developer Deploying Models
use_cases.description: Register trained models, configure inference endpoints, manage model versions, and integrate with Azure services.

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with Azure ML
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new Azure ML workspace. I need to connect to my Azure subscription, create a workspace in East US, and configure basic authentication using environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Register Training Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to register a dataset stored in Azure Blob Storage as a data asset in Azure ML. Include versioning and proper path configuration for the workspaceblobstore.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Compute for Training
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an AML compute cluster configuration with auto-scaling between 0 and 4 nodes, using Standard_DS3_v2 VMs, with idle scale-down after 2 hours of inactivity.

prompt_templates.title: Submit and Monitor Training Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me submit a command job that runs train.py with data and learning rate parameters. Include the code to stream logs and retrieve the job's studio URL for monitoring.

output_examples.input: Set up authentication and ML client connection
output: Configure AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID, AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP, and AZURE_ML_WORKSPACE_NAME environment variables
Use DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity or CLI authentication
Initialize MLClient with credential and workspace identifiers

output_examples.input: Create and submit a training job
output: Define command job with code directory, training script, and input parameters
Specify environment and compute target for job execution
Submit job and retrieve studio URL for real-time monitoring

best_practices: Enable auto-scaling on compute clusters with min_instances=0 to reduce costs during idle periods
Version all data assets, models, and environments to ensure reproducible experiments
Apply consistent tags to resources for cost allocation and organizational tracking
anti_patterns: Hardcoding credentials instead of using DefaultAzureCredential or environment variables
Creating compute clusters without idle scale-down configuration, leading to unnecessary costs
Skipping model and data versioning, making experiments impossible to reproduce

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure ML without storing secrets?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identities, Azure CLI login, and Visual Studio credentials. For local development, run 'az login' before executing your code.

faq.question: What is the difference between MLClient.from_config() and direct initialization?
faq.answer: from_config() reads workspace settings from a config.json file, while direct initialization requires explicit subscription_id, resource_group_name, and workspace_name parameters.

faq.question: How can I reduce compute costs for infrequent training jobs?
faq.answer: Set min_instances=0 on compute clusters and configure idle_time_before_scale_down. The cluster scales to zero when idle and only provisions resources when jobs are submitted.

faq.question: Can I track multiple model versions in the same registry?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure ML automatically versions models by name. Each create_or_update call with the same name increments the version number, enabling easy rollback and comparison.

faq.question: How do I monitor a training job after submission?
faq.answer: Use ml_client.jobs.stream(job_name) to view real-time logs in your terminal, or access the studio_url property to open the job in Azure ML Studio's web interface.

faq.question: What environments are available for training jobs?
faq.answer: Azure ML provides curated environments like 'AzureML-sklearn-1.0-ubuntu20.04-py38-cpu'. You can also create custom environments with specific conda or pip dependencies.
